---
title: "Notes S-01/Excel 1: Basics, formulas, charts, conditionals"
session_id: session-01
event_id: null
---

::: {.callout-important title="TODO"}
- Include screenshots from `Solutions.pptx`!
- Anfang: Worksheet (PR)
- Excel: Funktionsparametertrenner (komma/semikolon) kann nur Global umgestellt werden, beides zulässig.
- p.12: solution/dataset?
- **TODO: p.21: how can I open the dialogue?**
:::


| Time                 | Topic/activity                                             | Materials                                                            |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Part 1: 0–15 min** | Introduction and organization                              | Separate introductory slides                                         |
| 15–35 min            | Excel basics, formulas, and cell references                | Slides 1–6<br>[Excelsheet](../materials/introduction.xlsx)           |
| 35–55 min            | Future-value calculation                                   | Slides 7–10<br>Slides 7 and 10: Exercise **Future value**            |
| 55–70 min            | Data handling and chart selection                          | Slides 11–14<br>Slide 14: Exercise **Coffee-shop charts**            |
| 70–90 min            | Functions and descriptive statistics                       | Slides 15–19<br>Slide 19: Exercise **Sales statistics**              |
| **Break**            |                                                            |                                                                      |
| **Part 2: 0–15 min** | Explain the moving-average task and expected output        | Slide 20<br>Take-home exercise **DAX moving averages**               |
| 15–35 min            | Conditional logic and customer classification              | Slides 21–23<br>Slide 23: Exercise **VIP status**                    |
| 35–50 min            | Conditional calculations                                   | Slide 24: Exercise **VIP club credits**                              |
| 50–70 min            | Work through the installment-loan calculation on the board | Slide 25: Take-home exercise **Installment loan**                    |
| 70–82 min            | Annuity loans and the `PMT` function                       | Slide 26: Exercise **Annuity loan**                                  |
| 82–90 min            | Exam-result classification and wrap-up                     | Slides 27–28<br>Slide 27: Exercise **Exam result**                   |

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Slide 1 - Excel basics

Use [Excelsheet](../materials/introduction.xlsx) (see [solution](../materials/introduction_with_solutions.xlsx))

## Slide 17 — Formula syntax and functions

- Demonstrate the function-argument separator.
- Test with `SUM(3,5)`: everyone should have `8` as the result (**TODO: may need to update the Excel configuration**).
- Explicitly distinguish between a **formula** and a **function**.

## Slide 20 — DAX moving averages

- First take-home exercise.
- Explain the required moving averages and expected charts carefully before students begin.

**TODO: include illustration here, explain the problem well!**


## Slides 23–24 — VIP classification and credits

- Prefer nested `IF` functions for the VIP-status exercise (in part II).


## Slide 25 — Installment loan

- Work through the repayment logic on the board.
- Students then transfer the calculation to Excel as a take-home exercise.
- From period 2 onward, repayment requires an `IF` condition.
- Students should identify the need for the condition themselves.
- Allow sufficient explanation because this exercise has caused difficulties in previous cohorts.

`The repayment equals the fixed repayment amount or, when the remaining debt is lower, the remaining debt.`

**TODO: include visualization here**

::: {.callout-note title="Pro tips"}
- Use keyboard instead of clicking (prepare/mention shortcuts)
- Relative/absolute references: debug formula (predecessor/successor visualization)
:::

## Notes for improvement?

-> Separate paper



<!--
    ## Session overview

    - **Focus:** Excel
    - **Session title:** Excel 1
    - **Coverage target:** TBD

    ## Before class

    - [ ] Review the session slides/materials.
    - [ ] Prepare files, datasets, and examples.
    - [ ] Note any announcements or reminders.

    ## Timing record

    Use this table while preparing or after teaching to record the actual timing.

    | Planned time | Actual time | Topic/activity | Notes for next time |
    |---|---|---|---|
    | 0--10 min |  | Setup / recap |  |
    | 10--30 min |  | Main input |  |
    | 30--60 min |  | Guided practice |  |
    | 60--80 min |  | Independent practice / questions |  |
    | 80--90 min |  | Wrap-up |  |

    ## Notes during class

    - TBD

    ## Follow-up

    - [ ] Update examples or instructions.
    - [ ] Record common questions.
    - [ ] Add reminders for the next session.
-->
